canonical_question: What are the concurrency and parallelism patterns by language?
aliases: goroutines vs virtual threads vs asyncio
event loop vs thread pool vs coroutines

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Python 3.13 free-threaded build (experimental GIL removal, 2024-10); Java 21 virtual threads GA (2023-09)

constraints: Python's GIL prevents true parallelism with threads for CPU-bound work; use multiprocessing or the experimental free-threaded build (3.13+)
Node.js worker_threads share no JS objects by default; only ArrayBuffer/SharedArrayBuffer can be transferred
Go goroutines on a single GOMAXPROCS=1 setting run concurrently but not in parallel
Java virtual threads must not be pooled; create a new virtual thread per task
Rust async functions are lazy; they do nothing until polled by a runtime (tokio, async-std)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need distributed parallelism across multiple machines

inputs_needed.question: Is your workload I/O-bound (network, disk, database) or CPU-bound (computation, data processing)?
What programming language are you using?
